title: Octopus API Gateway Configuration
description: Configuration schema for the Octopus API Gateway

gateway.description: Core gateway settings including listen address, timeouts, TLS, and compression

upstreams.description: Upstream backend services to route traffic to

routes.description: Route definitions mapping paths to upstream services

plugins.description: Plugin configurations for extending gateway functionality

farp.description: FARP (Forge API Registration Protocol) service discovery and auto-routing

observability.description: Logging, metrics, and distributed tracing settings

auth_providers.description: Named authentication provider definitions keyed by provider name

Duration.description: A duration string consisting of a number followed by a time unit suffix (ms, s, m, h, d)

GatewayConfig.description: Core gateway configuration controlling the listener, worker threads, timeouts, TLS, and compression

workers.description: Number of worker threads (0 = auto-detect based on CPU cores)

request_timeout.description: Maximum duration for a single request before timeout

shutdown_timeout.description: Graceful shutdown timeout to wait for in-flight requests to complete

max_body_size.description: Maximum request body size in bytes

tls.description: Optional TLS configuration for HTTPS and mutual TLS

internal_route_prefix.description: Prefix for internal endpoints (admin, metrics, FARP). Endpoints become e.g. /__admin, /__metrics

TlsConfig.description: TLS configuration for HTTPS termination and optional mutual TLS (mTLS)

cert_file.description: Path to the TLS certificate file (PEM format)

key_file.description: Path to the TLS private key file (PEM format)

client_ca_file.description: Path to the CA certificate file for verifying client certificates (mTLS)

require_client_cert.description: Require client certificates for mutual TLS authentication

enable_cert_reload.description: Enable automatic reloading of TLS certificates when files change on disk

reload_interval_secs.description: Interval in seconds between certificate file change checks

CompressionConfig.description: Response compression configuration for reducing bandwidth usage

level.description: Compression level (1-9 for gzip/zstd, 1-11 for brotli)

min_size.description: Minimum response body size in bytes before compression is applied

algorithms.description: Preferred compression algorithms in priority order

UpstreamConfig.description: Configuration for an upstream backend service with load balancing, health checks, and circuit breaking

name.description: Unique name identifying this upstream service

instances.description: List of backend instances for this upstream

lb_policy.description: Load balancing policy for distributing requests across instances

health_check.description: Optional health check configuration for monitoring instance availability

circuit_breaker.description: Optional circuit breaker to prevent cascading failures

InstanceConfig.description: A single backend instance within an upstream service

id.description: Unique identifier for this instance within its upstream

host.description: Hostname or IP address of the backend instance

port.description: Port number the backend instance is listening on

weight.description: Relative weight for weighted load balancing (higher = more traffic)

metadata.description: Arbitrary key-value metadata for this instance (e.g., region, version)

HealthCheckConfig.description: Health check configuration for monitoring upstream instance availability

path.description: HTTP path for health check requests (only used when type is 'http')

interval.description: Time between consecutive health check probes

timeout.description: Maximum time to wait for a health check response

healthy_threshold.description: Number of consecutive successful checks required to mark an instance as healthy

unhealthy_threshold.description: Number of consecutive failed checks required to mark an instance as unhealthy

CircuitBreakerConfig.description: Circuit breaker configuration to prevent cascading failures by halting requests to failing upstreams

error_threshold.description: Error rate threshold (0.0 to 1.0) that triggers the circuit breaker to open

min_requests.description: Minimum number of requests within the window before the circuit breaker can activate

timeout.description: Duration the circuit breaker remains open before transitioning to half-open state

RouteConfig.description: Route definition mapping an HTTP path pattern to an upstream service with optional auth, rate limiting, and CORS

path.description: URL path pattern for matching requests (must start with '/'). Supports wildcards and path parameters

methods.description: Allowed HTTP methods for this route. Empty array means all methods are allowed

upstream.description: Name of the upstream service to route matching requests to

priority.description: Route matching priority (higher values are matched first)

strip_prefix.description: Prefix to strip from the request path before forwarding to the upstream

add_prefix.description: Prefix to add to the request path before forwarding to the upstream

metadata.description: Arbitrary key-value metadata associated with this route

auth_provider.description: Authentication provider name to use for this route (overrides the global default_provider)

skip_auth.description: Skip authentication for this route even when global enforcement is enabled

require_roles.description: List of roles that the authenticated principal must have to access this route

require_scopes.description: List of OAuth scopes that the authenticated principal must have to access this route

authz_rule.description: Custom authorization rule as a Rhai expression evaluated at request time

timeout.description: Per-route request timeout override (takes precedence over gateway-level request_timeout)

RouteRateLimitConfig.description: Per-route rate limiting using a sliding window

requests_per_window.description: Maximum number of requests allowed within the window period

window_size.description: Duration of the rate limit sliding window

RouteCorsConfig.description: Per-route CORS configuration that overrides the global CORS settings

allowed_origins.description: List of allowed origins for cross-origin requests

allowed_methods.description: List of allowed HTTP methods for cross-origin requests

allowed_headers.description: List of allowed request headers for cross-origin requests

allow_credentials.description: Whether to allow credentials (cookies, authorization headers) in cross-origin requests

max_age.description: Maximum time in seconds that preflight response can be cached by the browser

PluginConfig.description: Configuration for a gateway plugin that extends request/response processing

plugin_type.description: Plugin loading type: 'static' for built-in plugins, 'dynamic' for dynamically loaded plugins

enabled.description: Whether the plugin is enabled and should be loaded

priority.description: Plugin execution priority (higher values run first in the request chain)

config.description: Plugin-specific configuration as arbitrary key-value pairs

FarpConfig.description: FARP (Forge API Registration Protocol) configuration for automatic service discovery and route registration

enabled.description: Enable FARP service discovery and auto-routing

watch_interval.description: Interval between service discovery polling cycles

schema_cache_ttl.description: Time-to-live for cached service schemas before re-fetching

backends.description: List of discovery backends to use for finding services

DiscoveryBackendConfig.description: Configuration for a single discovery backend (mDNS, DNS, Consul, or Kubernetes)

enabled.description: Whether this discovery backend is enabled

AuthProviderConfig.description: Authentication provider definition, discriminated by the 'type' property

JwtAuthProvider.description: JWT token validation provider using static keys (HMAC, RSA, or ECDSA)

secret.description: HMAC shared secret for HS256/HS384/HS512 algorithms

public_key.description: RSA or ECDSA public key in PEM format (inline string)

public_key_file.description: Path to a file containing the RSA or ECDSA public key in PEM format

algorithm.description: JWT signing algorithm used to validate tokens

issuer.description: Expected 'iss' (issuer) claim value in the JWT

audience.description: Expected 'aud' (audience) claim value in the JWT

header_name.description: HTTP header name to extract the JWT token from

token_prefix.description: Prefix to strip from the header value before parsing the token

OidcAuthProvider.description: OpenID Connect provider with auto-discovery of JWKS endpoints and automatic key rotation

issuer_url.description: OIDC issuer URL used for .well-known/openid-configuration discovery

audience.description: Expected audience claim in the ID token

jwks_refresh_interval.description: Interval between JWKS key set refresh fetches from the issuer

required_scopes.description: OAuth scopes that must be present in the token for authentication to succeed

header_name.description: HTTP header name to extract the token from

token_prefix.description: Prefix to strip from the header value before parsing the token

fallback_provider.description: Name of a fallback auth provider to use when OIDC discovery fails

ApiKeyAuthProvider.description: API key authentication provider supporting static keys and external validation

header_name.description: HTTP header name to extract the API key from

query_param.description: Optional query parameter name to extract the API key from as a fallback

keys.description: List of statically configured API keys

external_validator.description: URL of an external API key validation service (receives POST with key in body)

ApiKeyEntry.description: A single static API key entry with associated identity and permissions

name.description: Human-readable name identifying the key holder

scopes.description: List of scopes granted to this API key

rate_limit.description: Per-key rate limit in requests per minute

ForwardAuthProvider.description: Forward authentication provider that delegates auth decisions to an external service

endpoint.description: URL of the external authentication service

forward_headers.description: Headers to forward from the original request to the auth service

response_headers.description: Headers to copy from the auth service response back into the upstream request

timeout.description: Timeout for the forward auth HTTP request

cache_ttl.description: Duration to cache auth responses keyed by token hash

MtlsAuthProvider.description: Mutual TLS (mTLS) authentication provider using client certificates

client_ca_file.description: Path to the CA certificate file for verifying client certificates

require_client_cert.description: Whether to require a valid client certificate for every request

extract_cn_as_principal.description: Extract the certificate Common Name (CN) as the authenticated principal identity

cn_to_roles.description: Mapping of certificate CN patterns to role arrays for role-based access control

AuthConfig.description: Global authentication and authorization settings applied across all routes

default_provider.description: Name of the default auth provider applied to routes without an explicit auth_provider

global_enforce.description: Enforce authentication on all routes globally (routes must set skip_auth to opt out)

skip_paths.description: URL path patterns to exclude from authentication (supports wildcards)

principal_header.description: Header name to inject the authenticated principal identity into upstream requests

roles_header.description: Header name to inject the authenticated principal roles into upstream requests

scopes_header.description: Header name to inject the authenticated scopes into upstream requests

token_cache_ttl.description: Duration to cache validated token results to avoid re-validation on every request

AuthzConfig.description: Authorization configuration for evaluating access control rules using Rhai scripts and/or OPA

engine.description: Authorization engine to use for evaluating rules

global_rules.description: Global authorization rules applied to all authenticated requests

opa.description: Open Policy Agent (OPA) integration settings (required when engine is 'opa' or 'both')

AuthzRule.description: A single authorization rule evaluated against the request and authenticated principal

name.description: Human-readable name for identifying this rule in logs and errors

description.description: Optional human-readable description of what this rule enforces
examples: Ensures only admin users can access management endpoints

rule.description: Authorization rule expression (Rhai script or OPA policy path depending on engine)

action.description: Action to take when the rule expression evaluates to true

OpaConfig.description: Open Policy Agent (OPA) integration configuration for external policy evaluation

endpoint.description: OPA REST API endpoint URL for policy evaluation

timeout.description: Maximum time to wait for an OPA policy evaluation response

cache_ttl.description: Duration to cache OPA policy decisions

fail_open.description: Allow requests to proceed when the OPA service is unreachable (fail-open vs fail-closed)

CorsGlobalConfig.description: Global CORS (Cross-Origin Resource Sharing) configuration applied to all routes unless overridden

allowed_origins.description: List of allowed origins for cross-origin requests (use ["*"] with caution)

allowed_methods.description: List of allowed HTTP methods in cross-origin requests

allowed_headers.description: List of allowed request headers in cross-origin requests

exposed_headers.description: List of response headers that browsers are allowed to access in cross-origin responses

max_age.description: Maximum time in seconds that the browser may cache preflight responses

allow_credentials.description: Whether to allow credentials (cookies, authorization headers) in cross-origin requests

auth_provider.description: Name of the authentication provider to protect the admin dashboard (omit for no auth)

allowed_ips.description: IP allowlist for admin dashboard access (empty means all IPs are allowed)

ObservabilityConfig.description: Observability configuration for logging, metrics, and distributed tracing

LoggingConfig.description: Logging configuration for controlling output verbosity and format

endpoint.description: HTTP path where Prometheus metrics are exposed

TracingConfig.description: Distributed tracing configuration for OpenTelemetry-compatible backends

jaeger_endpoint.description: Jaeger collector endpoint URL for sending trace spans

GrpcConfig.description: gRPC gateway configuration for proxying gRPC traffic to upstream services

max_message_size.description: Maximum gRPC message size in bytes

enable_reflection.description: Enable gRPC server reflection proxy for service introspection

enable_grpc_web.description: Enable gRPC-Web support for HTTP/1.1 compatible gRPC calls from browsers

deadline_propagation.description: Propagate gRPC deadlines from client requests to upstream services

services.description: Explicit mapping of fully qualified gRPC service names to upstream names

additionalProperties.description: Upstream name that handles this gRPC service
